title: In a significant legislative victory, Senate Bill (SB) 268, authored by Senator Marie Alvarado-Gil (R-Jackson), has successfully passed the Assembly floor with 74 “AYE” votes. This

claims.claim_text: Senate Bill 268 aims to close a legal loophole that fails to recognize the violent nature of the rape of an unconscious person.
Under current law, the rape of an intoxicated person is not classified as a violent felony.
Current legislation allows individuals convicted of non-violent felonies, including the rape of an intoxicated person, to be eligible for early prison release.
Senate Bill 268 seeks to classify drugging and rape as violent assaults.
The overwhelming support for SB 268 reflects the urgency of recognizing the rape of an intoxicated person as a violent felony.
